name: Sodium hydroxide
description: Sodium hydroxide is an inorganic compound with the chemical formula NaOH in the form of white solids. With several hydrate forms available, it is a strong base used in various chemical, industrial, and commercial applications, including the manufacture of sodium salts and detergents, pH regulation, and organic synthesis [Wikipedia]. It is also used in chemical pulping processes and industrial cleaning agents. Upon direct contact on human skin and eyes, sodium hydroxide can cause serious chemical burns like other corrosive alkaline compounds. Protective equipments when handling the compound is strongly advised and in case of direct contact with skin or eyes, the exposure should be discontinued immediately and the area of contact should be washed throughly with large amounts of water. By the FDA, sodium hydroxide is considered a direct food substance affirmed as generally recognized as safe, where it serves as a pH control agent.
